characters.name: Achille
characters.role: The narrator's brother, who restrains him from acting impulsively.
characters.citation: sentii la mano ferrea di mio fratello Achille che mi tratteneva.

characters.name: Radetzky
characters.role: A military commander who gave orders for garrisons to contract and for the evacuation of territories.
characters.citation: Radetzky aveva dato ordine alle guarnigioni stanziate qua e là nelle varie parti del Regno

characters.name: de Domini
characters.role: The narrator's maternal uncle, an 'austriacante arrabbiato' (enraged pro-Austrian).
characters.citation: La guarnigione di Brescia, capitanata da un de Domini, mio zio materno

characters.name: Carlo Alberto
characters.role: A figure whose delay is criticized, and who incited resistance against Radetzky's retreat.
characters.citation: Se Carlo Alberto, invece di perdere un tempo prezioso brigando per soddisfare le esigenze della Diplomazia

events.name: Primo tuffo nella vita intensa
events.description: The narrator's first decisive immersion into an intense life, coinciding with the retreat of German troops from bastions.
events.citation: Il giorno stesso della nostra fuga, ebbi il primo tuffo nella vita intensa... e doveva essere decisivo.

events.name: Sgombro forzato dei tedeschi
events.description: The forced evacuation of German (Austrian) troops from bastions, observed by the narrator.
events.citation: assistevamo, a piè fermo, a quel loro sgombro forzato.

events.name: Uccisione del bambino
events.description: A Croatian soldier bayonets a serene child playing near the bastions.
events.citation: La baionetta penetrò nel fianco e uscì dalla schiena di quell'innocente

events.name: Uccisione della madre incinta
events.description: The child's pregnant mother, witnessing her son's death, is also bayonetted and dies, falling with her unborn child.
events.citation: gli aguzzini d'Italia vibrarono un'altra baionettata ed ella precipitava riversa, cadavere con un cadavere in seno.

events.name: Formazione della persuasione ribelle
events.description: After witnessing atrocities, the narrator develops the conviction that salvation for the wretched lies only in awakening their rebellious energies, advocating action over prayer.
events.citation: nel mio cuore incominciò a farsi strada la persuasione che nessuna salvezza hanno i derelitti fuorché nel risveglio delle proprie energie ribelli.

events.name: Ritirata di Radetzky
events.description: Radetzky ordered his garrisons to converge with the main army and evacuate all towns on his path, threatening resistance with artillery and plunder.
events.citation: La nostra ritirata da Milano a Verona non fu di tal natura da affievolire quella terribile impressione.

events.name: Saccheggio di Melegnano
events.description: Melegnano, attempting to block the enemy, was subdued by fifty pieces of artillery and subsequently plundered by drunken and savage Croatian soldiers.
events.citation: Melegnano, che si era lusingata d'intercettare la strada al nemico, fu tosto ridotta in soggezione da cinquanta pezzi d'artiglieria.

events.name: Discussione con de Domini
events.description: The narrator had a heated political argument with his pro-Austrian uncle, de Domini, in Desenzano, expressing strong anti-Austrian sentiments.
events.citation: Mi espressi con linguaggio vibrato e libero. Il vecchio militare impallidì e mi interruppe.

events.name: Saccheggio di Castelnuovo
events.description: The small town of Castelnuovo attempted to barricade itself against Radetzky's retreat but was quickly overcome by imperial artillery, then abandoned to the soldiers' lust.
events.citation: L'artiglieria imperiale ebbe presto ragione del patriottismo italiano. Castelnuovo crivellata, in fiamme, venne abbandonata alla libidine della soldatesca.

events.name: Uccisione della giovinetta a Castelnuovo
events.description: A young, sick girl fleeing a burning house in Castelnuovo was shot by a soldier while the narrator attempted to rescue her.
events.citation: Un altro soldataccio... spianò la carabina e fece fuoco. La giovinetta cadde e una striscia scarlatta rigò la candida tela che l'avvolgeva.

events.name: Giuramento di vendetta
events.description: Deeply affected by the girl's death, the narrator felt a powerful surge of new resolve, viewing her martyrdom as symbolic of the Homeland's suffering and reinforcing his commitment to Liberty.
events.citation: Io ne soffersi come se accogliessi nell'animo tutti quanti i dolori della Patria e un impeto di propositi nuovi si sprigionò improvvisamente dal profondo.

events.name: Primo sangue versato dal narratore e Achille
events.description: That night, the narrator and Achille shed blood in an act they considered justice and defense of their rising race, avenging the murdered Lombard mother and the girl from Castelnuovo.
events.citation: Fu quella notte che le mie mani e quelle d'Achille conobbero il primo sangue. Atto in difesa della mia razza risorgente, atto di Giustizia!

locations.name: Porta San Celso
locations.context: One of the bastions where German troops were retreating from.
locations.citation: bastioni che corrono da Porta San Celso a Porta Romana

locations.name: Porta Romana
locations.context: One of the bastions where German troops were retreating towards.
locations.citation: bastioni che corrono da Porta San Celso a Porta Romana

locations.name: Milano
locations.context: The starting point of the narrator's retreat with Achille.
locations.citation: La nostra ritirata da Milano a Verona

locations.name: Verona
locations.context: The destination of the narrator's retreat; also a strong refuge for Radetzky's forces.
locations.citation: La nostra ritirata da Milano a Verona

locations.name: Regno
locations.context: Refers to the Lombardy-Venetia Kingdom, where Radetzky's garrisons were stationed.

locations.name: Lodi
locations.context: A location where the Imperial forces arrived, showing signs of confusion and panic.
locations.citation: giunti a mala pena a Lodi

locations.name: Ticino
locations.context: A river that, if Carlo Alberto had crossed quickly, could have led to Austria's defeat.

locations.name: Melegnano
locations.context: A town that attempted to intercept the enemy but was besieged and plundered.
locations.citation: Melegnano, che si era lusingata d'intercettare la strada al nemico

locations.name: Desenzano
locations.context: The place where the narrator met his uncle de Domini and had a political dispute.
locations.citation: Esso ebbe un primo scoppio a Desenzano.

locations.name: Brescia
locations.context: The location from which de Domini's garrison came.

locations.name: Peschiera
locations.context: A location the army had passed before hearing about Castelnuovo's resistance.
locations.citation: L'esercito aveva lasciato alle spalle Peschiera

locations.name: Castelnuovo
locations.context: A small town that bravely attempted to barricade itself against Radetzky, resulting in its destruction and the death of a young girl.

relationships.citation: sentii la mano ferrea di mio fratello Achille che mi tratteneva.
capitanata da un de Domini, mio zio materno
Saputo della presenza dei due nipoti, volle vederli.

terminology.meaning: Germans, used here to refer to the Austrian troops.
terminology.citation: Sfilavano i tedeschi sui bastioni che corrono da Porta San Celso a Porta Romana

terminology.meaning: The Italian national flag, a symbol of rebellion against Austrian rule.
terminology.citation: i tedeschi mostravano il pugno al tricolore che sventolava sul suo pinnacolo maggiore.

terminology.meaning: To ring bells in a loud, continuous, and irregular manner, typically as an alarm or signal of danger/uprising.
terminology.citation: Le campane continuavano a suonare a stormo.

terminology.meaning: Croatians, soldiers serving in the Austrian imperial army, often depicted as brutal.
terminology.citation: ogni Compagnia preceduta da tre Croati che facevano da battistrada.

terminology.meaning: Executioners or tormentors of Italy, a strong derogatory term for the oppressors.
terminology.citation: gli aguzzini d'Italia vibrarono un'altra baionettata ed ella precipitava riversa

terminology.meaning: Homeland, nation, used with strong patriotic connotation.
terminology.citation: Io ne soffersi come se accogliessi nell'animo tutti quanti i dolori della Patria

terminology.meaning: Relating to the Austrian Empire or its forces.
terminology.citation: La confusione, il panico degli Imperiali erano tali che, giunti a mala pena a Lodi

terminology.meaning: Pro-Austrian, someone who supports or favors the Austrian side.
terminology.citation: Era lo zio un austriacante arrabbiato ed io non lo ignoravo.

terminology.meaning: Executioner, hangman; used here to represent the ultimate penalty or danger.
terminology.citation: o tosto o tardi lascerai la testa tra le mani del carnefice!

terminology.meaning: Military cadets from an academy; refers to the narrator and his companions.
terminology.citation: Noi, cadetti dell'Accademia, si assisteva a quello scempio schierati presso la porta delle vecchie mura

terminology.meaning: Rising, re-emerging race or people; referring to the Italian nation/people in the process of achieving independence and unity (Risorgimento).
terminology.citation: Atto in difesa della mia razza risorgente, atto di Giustizia!

failed.name: Uccisione della giovinetta a Castelnuovo
failed.citation_preview: Un altro soldataccio... spianò la carabina e fece fuoco. La giovinetta cadde e u
